architecture.summary: SPLARE trains Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) to decompose LLM representations into interpretable latent features for Learned Sparse Retrieval. Queries and documents are encoded into high-dimensional sparse representations by projecting LLM activations through a trained SAE, producing semantically structured features for efficient retrieval. The method operates by training an SAE decoder to map from dense LLM hidden states to sparse latent codes, which replace vocabulary-space projections used in prior LSR work.
trained_components: SAE encoder and decoder for sparse feature extraction
Query and document projection layers into SAE latent space

biblio.title: Learning Retrieval Models with Sparse Autoencoders

claims.statement: SAE-based representations offer the potential to produce more semantically structured, expressive, and language-agnostic features compared to vocabulary-based LSR approaches
SPLARE consistently outperforms vocabulary-based LSR in multilingual and out-of-domain settings
SPLARE-7B achieves top results on MMTEB's multilingual and English retrieval tasks
A 2B-parameter variant was developed with a significantly lighter footprint
